item.title: How does a majority government work? What to know as Carney nears threshold
item.description: While crossing the majority threshold could make Carney's political life easier, there will still be ways for the opposition, the public and even Liberals MPs to have influence.

item.title: Saab dangles sovereign data centre in Montreal to undercut F-35 fighter contract
item.description: Saab is pitching a Montreal-based, sovereign data hub to secure Canada's fighter jet contract, arguing it would keep mission-critical data out of U.S. hands. The move challenges Lockheed Martin's F-35 model and amplifies Ottawa's growing unease over data control, AI and strategic dependence.

item.title: Immigration Department informing some 30,000 applicants they may be ineligible for refugee hearings
item.description: Canada's Immigration Department is sending tens of thousands of refugee claimants letters that they may not be eligible for asylum — and is telling some of them that they should leave immediately.

item.title: Making tracks for the mayor’s office? Scarborough transit takes centre stage at city hall
Ontario to overhaul teacher training in move to fix educator shortage
Internal memo flags the promise and pitfalls of expanding CSIS’s foreign spy role
item.description: The Canadian Press used the Access to Information Act to obtain the memo, which was drafted by CSIS in advance of a roundtable meeting organiz…

item.title: Why Olivia Chow loves ‘Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie’ — even if the film broke city bylaws
item.description: The mayor says the Toronto friends behind the film that premiered at 2025 TIFF may have broken at least 10 bylaws. "But that's OK."

item.title: Why a Toronto sign telling drivers to ‘Stop Here’ shows poor judgment by the city
Sex assault trial of Ontario billionaire Frank Stronach ends with three of seven complainants left. Judge set to rule by June
PM Mark Carney visits Canadiens’ dressing room
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney visited the Montreal Canadiens dressing room after the team's 2-1 win over the Tampa Bay Lightning in a thrilling game Thursday night.

item.title: Gladu says crossing floor to Liberals was ‘best thing’ for her riding, herself
item.description: Gladu stole the show at the Liberal convention after she stunned political circles by unexpectedly crossing the floor from the Tories earlier this week.

item.title: Senators vote to update definition of pornography so films with sex scenes aren’t affected by age verification bill
item.description: Bill requiring porn sites take steps to check that users are at least 18 is expected to clear Senate next week

item.title: A Liberal Party in a triumphal mood finds it easier to accept conflicting values
item.description: The values to be highlighted at Carney’s convention are noticeably different from the ones seen at Trudeau’s event in 2023

item.title: Small businesses bear the brunt of rising crime, national survey says
item.description: From vandalism to public drug use, owners say safety concerns are driving customers away and cutting into their bottom line

item.title: Poilievre challenges PM Carney to defend private property rights in B.C. in wake of Cowichan decision
item.description: Federal Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is calling on Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal government to defend private property rights in face of what he claims is a threat posed by a landmark B.C. Supreme Court decision establishing Aboriginal title on private property.

item.title: Alberta separatists claim looming Carney majority helps their cause
item.description: Alberta separatists in Alberta claim recent floor crossings in Ottawa and the prospect of a federal Liberal majority government will be a boost to their efforts for independence.

item.title: Floor-crosser Marilyn Gladu says she’ll toe Liberal party line despite her past positions on social issues
Trump rages, NATO endures: Why the alliance is harder to kill than it looks
item.description: Despite threats from U.S. President Donald Trump, an American break with NATO remains unlikely. Political constraints, military dependence and mutual interests bind both sides. The real danger involves eroding trust, emboldening Russia and a slow-motion fracture that weakens deterrence without ever triggering a formal divorce.

item.title: Ontario electricity system to add 14 new solar, wind projects
Pierre Poilievre defiant after fourth Conservative MP defects to Liberals
Politics Insider: Pierre Poilievre has big feelings about floor crossers
item.description: Plus, first ‘nation-building’ project begins construction and Carney takes a call from space

item.title: Who isn’t welcome in Mark Carney’s technicolour Liberal caucus?
item.description: The prime minister has drawn floor crossers that range from an NDP MP that backed Avi Lewis to a conservative that wanted December to be known as 'Christian Heritage Month.'

item.title: GTHA transit unions call on Ford government to halt expansion of fare integration program
item.description: Local transit unions in the Greater Toronto and Hamilton Area are calling on the Doug Ford government not to push forward with its plan to harmonize fares across the region, saying they have serious concerns about the move.

item.title: After more than two decades, Toronto may finally allow more of this on its sidewalks
item.description: City staff are recommending changes to modernize Toronto's rules around permits for street vendors and street artists.

item.title: Poilievre fires back at questions about his leadership after fourth floor crossing
item.description: OTTAWA - Pierre Poilievre was defiant on Thursday in the face of questions about his leadership of the Conservative party after a fourth membe…

item.title: After five floor crossings, how close are the Liberals to a majority?
item.description: Marilyn Gladu's floor crossing in the House of Commons could have a big impact for the Liberals. The Globe’s Stephanie Levitz explains what’s needed for the party to achieve a majority.

item.title: Scarborough ‘left behind’ when it comes to public transit, Bradford says
item.description: Video

item.title: 'Throw them out' of Parliament: Poilievre in favour of recall petition for floor-crossers
item.description: After losing another of his MPs to the Liberals, Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre says he personally supports recall petitions — a way for constituents to remove a representative if they garner enough local support.

item.title: Longtime Conservative MP Marilyn Gladu joins Liberals in stunning floor crossing
Up to 40 Conservative MPs fear Pierre Poilievre will cost them their seats, insiders say
Why 173 seats — and not needing the Speaker’s vote — matters to the Liberals
Lewis says Gladu’s floor-crossing shows Parliament needs the NDP
Mark Carney says Mideast ceasefire ‘needs to include Lebanon’
Ontario unveils plans for more power generation from renewable energy projects
item.description: The projects are slated to come online in 2030 and power the equivalent of 350,000 homes in what Energy Minister Stephen Lecce said is the pro…

item.title: Floor-crossers support core Liberal values, Carney says
item.description: MPs who defect to the Liberal caucus support the party's platform, Prime Minister Mark Carney said when asked about the positions of opposition parliamentarians from across the political spectrum who have joined the red tent in recent months.

item.title: Doug Ford shrugs off floor-crossings in Ottawa: ‘That’s up to the local MPs’
Canada seeking observer role in sixth generation fighter jet project
item.description: The push to join the Global Combat Air Programme, even as an observer, is part of efforts to diversify defence ties, says senior government official

item.title: Carney says floor-crossing MPs must support Liberal values on abortion, LGBTQ rights
Marilyn Gladu will vote with Liberals on abortion, Carney says
item.description: 'She will vote with the government if there are votes relating to any aspect of that issue as well as the rights of Canadians to be their whole selves,' Carney said.

item.title: Latest defection puts Mark Carney on the verge of a majority government
Carney says Liberal values are unchanged after socially conservative MP joins party
item.description: Former Conservative Marilyn Gladu has criticized COVID-19 restrictions and supported bills that would curtail access to abortion

item.title: Canada pushing for Lebanon inclusion into ceasefire deal: source
item.description: Ottawa is pushing for Lebanon to be part of the ceasefire deal between the Trump administration and Iran and for its inclusion to be honoured by Israel, a senior Liberal source told CBC News.

item.title: Port expansion near Montreal is first ‘nation-building’ project to begin construction
item.description: The terminal is in Contrecœur, Que., about 45 kilometres northeast of Montreal

item.title: A long-awaited LRT to serve Toronto’s booming eastern waterfront is finally getting funding
Liberal convention welcomes Gladu, Carney with majority within reach
item.description: Liberal party national director Azam Ishmael boasted Thursday that the party expects a "record-breaking number" of supporters to attend the convention — around 4,500 members.

item.title: With Carney riding high, Liberals meet ahead of crucial by-elections
item.description: After floor-crossing by Conservative MP Gladu, Liberals appear poised to win a majority of seats in the House

item.title: Canada’s closest trading partners are pushing back against Carney’s Buy Canadian policy
item.description: The post Canada’s closest trading partners are pushing back against Carney’s Buy Canadian policy appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Marilyn Gladu says she's pro-choice, anti conversion therapy as Liberals embrace former Conservative
item.description: Once seen as a social conservative, floor-crosser Marilyn Gladu clarified some of her previous positions from the sidelines of the Liberal convention Thursday, saying she's pro-choice and anti conversion therapy.

item.title: Carney applauds Artemis II crew's 'inspiring' example of teamwork in space-to-Earth call
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney praised the Artemis II moon mission as a "unique example for the world and beyond" of what can be achieved through collaboration during a brief call with moon-mission's crew on Wednesday evening.

item.title: Deputy minister breached conflict of interest rules by intervening in hiring decision, says ethics watchdog
item.description: Senior public servant Christiane Fox influenced the hiring of a man she knew from when they were both student athletes, reports says

item.title: Carney's Liberals are attracting support from unlikely places
item.description: During his brief and ill-fated time as leader of the Liberal Party, Michael Ignatieff invited Canadians to join him inside the "big red tent." This appeal obviously did not work out for Ignatieff. But perhaps he was merely ahead of his time.

item.title: Politics Insider: The Liberals acquire a surprising new caucus colleague
item.description: Marilyn Gladu leaped from the right-most Tory flank to the government benches

item.title: Quebec immigration minister Roberge investigated for alleged ethics breach
item.description: Liberals and Québec solidaire are accusing Roberge of violating the part of the ethics code that bars elected officials from disclosing information

item.title: Quebec legislature prorogued as CAQ prepares to name new leader
item.description: Cabinet of outgoing Premier Francois Legault says national assembly will return with a new session — and new premier — on May 5

item.title: Environmental groups launch constitutional challenge over Ontario's special economic zones
item.description: Several environmental groups have launched a constitutional challenge seeking to kill an Ontario law that allows cabinet to suspend other laws, arguing the Doug Ford government has abdicated the role of the legislature.

item.title: Deputy minister broke rules by hiring unqualified acquaintance: ethics watchdog
item.description: Deputy minister of national defence Christiane Fox broke conflict of interest rules by hiring an old acquaintance when she was the deputy immigration minister, the federal ethics watchdog has found.

item.title: Iran tests the limits of sanctions with bitcoin tolls in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The post Iran tests the limits of sanctions with bitcoin tolls in Strait of Hormuz appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Mark Carney heads to Liberal convention with broad support
Ontario to extend WSIB coverage in privately run retirement and group homes
item.description: Ontario is planning to extend workplace safety and insurance coverage to health-care and support workers in privately run retirement homes and group homes.

item.title: A timeline of floor crossings from the opposition benches to Mark Carney’s Liberals
item.description: Marilyn Gladu became the fifth opposition member of Parliament to cross the floor to Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberals on Wednesday.

item.title: Scores will remain secret as Ford government funds more skills development applicants
Doug Ford’s Bill 5 called a ‘dangerously undemocratic’ law as legal challenge is launched
Doug Ford’s FOI moves driving down support for Progressive Conservatives, survey finds
Tax cuts not the only way to help Canadians as gas prices rise: economists
Toronto could temporarily close additional roads during FIFA World Cup
item.description: A city bureaucrat may be granted new powers to temporarily close roads during the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Housing minister’s office issues ‘correction’ after he says new home GST tax cut talks underway
item.description: Housing Minister Gregor Robertson’s office is backtracking on comments he made to Global News in which he had said Ottawa is in talks with provinces to cut GST on new build homes.

item.title: The Doug Ford government is moving to shield politicians from scrutiny. Here are secrets the new law would have kept under wraps
Ross Perot Jr. calls Doug Ford a ‘great salesman’ on Texas trip
item.description: The son of a former U.S. presidential candidate of the same name met the Ontario Premier last week

item.title: Carney is on the verge of a majority. These 3 byelections will decide
item.description: Carney announced federal byelections on April 13 in two Toronto-area ridings of Scarborough Southwest and University-Rosedale and the Montreal-area riding of Terrebonne.

item.title: Almost 4,000 federal public servants apply for early retirement program
Poilievre set to lose communications director seen as an agent of change
item.description: Katy Merrifield, 42, joined Poilievre's office last fall and quickly set about changing the Conservative leader's media strategy and communications approach.

item.title: Union claims airlines are 'flying the plane' in Ottawa's unpaid work probe
item.description: The Canadian Union of Public Employees says the federal government's probe into allegations of unpaid work in the airline sector is not taking workers' arguments seriously.

item.title: Former Conservative MP Marilyn Gladu crosses floor to Liberals
item.description: The Liberals have picked up another Conservative floor-crosser, bringing them one seat closer to a possible majority government after next week's byelections.

item.title: Advocates call for funding to ensure safety, prosperity for Indigenous women
item.description: Organizations holding a news conference on Parliament Hill today urge continued funds for support programs and services

item.title: 'Every vote counts': Liberals campaign hard for Quebec seat in Monday's federal byelection
item.description: Federal Liberals are throwing all they can at the Quebec riding of Terrebonne ahead of Monday's byelection, as a 173-seat majority remains a possibility.

item.title: EV giant BYD accused of forced labour violations at European factory
item.description: As Canada lowers its tariffs and imports more electric vehicles from China, an upcoming report from New York-based labour rights researchers is making new allegations of forced labour practices at the world's bestselling EV manufacturer, BYD.

item.title: How a Senate bill could end the Indian Act's 2nd-generation cut-off
item.description: For 150 years, who is considered a "Status Indian" in Canadian law has been determined by the federal government through the Indian Act. Now, a Senate bill could end the "second-generation cut-off." What would this mean for communities, and does the bill stand a chance of passing in the House?

item.title: As drones upend tank warfare, Canada's army races to rethink its armour
item.description: Canada's army is weighing faster timelines for new armoured vehicles while rethinking tanks in light of Ukraine's drone-dominated battlefield. Aging Leopards need replacement, but commanders say technology — including uncrewed systems — will shape decisions, even as other priorities like air defence and long-range strike rank higher.

item.title: AI-controlled traffic lights coming to Yonge Street as part of Toronto’s plan to ease gridlock
item.description: The City of Toronto is turning to artificial intelligence to unclog its congested streets. After a recent test, intersections in North York are being equipped with smart traffic signals.

item.title: Immigration officers don’t have latitude to probe refugee claims, experts say
item.description: Internal government documents suggest front-line officials are told to leave judgments on the validity of claims to others

item.title: Why Canada's reaction to Israel's invasion of Lebanon is so different this time
item.description: Canada's response to Israel's 2026 invasion of Lebanon has been unequivocal condemnation of what Prime Minister Mark Carney called "an illegal invasion." That's a marked contrast to Stephen Harper's defence of Israel's 2006 invasion.

item.title: Could this be the end of green building standards in Ontario — again?
item.description: Premier Doug Ford’s government is taking another swipe at green standards while nixing a requirement for municipalities to build climate change goals into their official plans.

item.title: After 150 years, Canada still has an Indian Act. Why is it so hard to get rid of?
item.description: The Indian Act marks 150 years since it was enacted this month. CBC Radio's The House spoke with First Nations leaders, historians and legal experts about the Indian Act and attempts by First Nations to move beyond it.

item.title: Conservatives call for ethics probe of Champagne as questions raised over high-speed rail
item.description: The Conservatives are calling for an ethics investigation into Finance Minister François-Philippe Champagne in regards to the involvement of his partner in the Alto high-speed rail project. Prime Minister Mark Carney defended Champagne, saying the minister followed all of the ethics rules.

item.title: Doug Ford defends FOI changes, warns of enemies seeking government secrets
Scarborough youth crime prevention programs get a last-minute reprieve from city council
Canada’s EV rebate hits a sweet spot in the auto market, says new report
item.description: The post Canada’s EV rebate hits a sweet spot in the auto market, says new report appeared first on The Logic .
